responseMessageContents.text:
The decrypted token shows as revoked when tested directly, but the Rork deploy bridge still reports it as present with the same mask — which means it would also fail with a 401 error. I've confirmed the token is revoked multiple times now.

I've satisfied the requirements for requesting a replacement: removed the placeholder from Render, redeployed the service, decrypted and tested the stored token against GitHub's API, and confirmed it returns a real 401 from the production runtime. Before escalating, I want to test the token directly from the production worker itself using the github-test action on the bridge, and also check if there's another GITHUB_TOKEN hiding in the Rork private environment variables — the private env list shows both GITHUB_TOKEN and RENDER_API_KEY, though the sandbox showed an empty list earlier.

The core issue is circular: the new code with the resolver fix can't deploy to Render because GitHub pushes are blocked by the revoked token, but I can't test the store-based approach from the old Render backend until the new code is deployed. I need to find a way to get the updated code to Render without relying on a GitHub push.

Let me run the production bridge github-test to capture the actual 401 error evidence from the runtime, then compile the final evidence block with the exact failure details and UNVERIFIED status. I should also grab the sanitized GitHub error message body to include in the evidence.

responseMessageContents.text:
The token file was cleaned up between commands, and the GitHub API test with the real token returned a 401 error as expected. All the section-19 conditions are satisfied now—the placeholder has been removed from Render and the deployment was redeployed successfully.

The resolver is fully corrected with all tests passing, the runtime has been restarted, and the stored secret binding was verified to have the correct format. Since the token is revoked at GitHub, I need to document this as the final state and prepare the evidence block for section 20, marking which items couldn't be completed without a fresh token.

RENDER_SERVICE_ID=srv-d7t9ivreo5us73ftose0
RENDER_SERVICE_NAME=ivx-holdings-platform
ACTIVE_ENVIRONMENT=production (branch: main, not_suspended)
PRODUCTION_URL=https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com

PLACEHOLDER_ENV_REMOVED=YES (DELETE /env-vars/GITHUB_TOKEN → HTTP 204, re-query → 0 GITHUB_TOKEN vars)
DUPLICATE_GITHUB_TOKEN_COUNT=0
SERVICE_RESTARTED=YES (deploy dep-d98qpe9o3t8c73ebd8b0 triggered 2026-07-11T02:38:49Z → status live)
PLACEHOLDER_REFERENCES_AFTER=0 executable (test fixtures only, isolated in `*.test.ts`)

CREDENTIAL_SOURCES_FOUND=render_env(deleted), owner_encrypted_store(Supabase ivx_owner_variables), cloudflare_bridge_worker_env, sandbox_env(all empty: GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_PAT/GITHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN/GITHUB_APP_TOKEN len:0)
ACTIVE_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=owner_encrypted_store (AES-256-GCM, decrypted OK, valid ghp_ shape, 40 chars, no whitespace/quotes/truncation)
PLACEHOLDER_SOURCE=render_env GITHUB_TOKEN (len 11, value PLACEHOLDER) — REMOVED

REAL_TOKEN_PRESENT=YES
REAL_TOKEN_SOURCE=owner_encrypted_store + cloudflare_bridge_worker (same credential in both)
REAL_TOKEN_RUNTIME_BOUND=PASS (production bridge worker read it: present:true, length:40, isPlaceholder:false)
PLACEHOLDER_DETECTED_AFTER_FIX=NO

GITHUB_AUTH_HTTP_STATUS=401
SANITIZED_GITHUB_ERROR=Bad credentials (GET /user, from production worker AND sandbox, identical result)
AUTHENTICATED_LOGIN=NONE — GitHub rejects the credential itself
REPOSITORY=ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest (exists, public, HEAD d35db8b9)
BRANCH=main
READ_ACCESS=YES (public) WRITE_ACCESS=NO PUSH_PERMISSION=NO
WRITE_TEST=FAIL (auth fails before any write is possible)

RESOLVER_ENFORCED=YES — order: owner-store → validated env; rejects PLACEHOLDER/MOCK/UNKNOWN/TEST/DUMMY/empty/malformed; fails with GITHUB_CREDENTIAL_NOT_CONFIGURED; wired into github-tool, credential-sync, production-evidence, enterprise-deployment-engine, chat-brain, autonomy, owner-variables runtime
TESTS=908 pass / 0 fail (resolver 13/13, orchestrator, state-machine 96/96, narrative gates, media integrity 9/9) TYPECHECK=clean
COMMIT_SHA=70e0af2976e6b834f7dd4d4c06a75519ffba3683 (workspace, all fixes included)
PUSH_STATUS=FAILED — HTTP 401 at authentication
DEPLOYMENT_STATUS=env-fix deploy live; code deploy blocked at PUSH_COMPLETED
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=d35db8b9 GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=d35db8b9 COMMIT_MATCH=YES (but both predate the fixes)
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=200
PEREZ_MEDIA_QA=FAIL CASA_ROSARIO_MEDIA_QA=FAIL OWNERSHIP_PARITY_QA=FAIL LANDING_REELS_QA=FAIL APP_REELS_QA=FAIL APP_LANDING_SYNC_QA=FAIL (fixes exist in commit 70e0af2, not on GitHub → not in production)

FINAL_STATUS=UNVERIFIED

**Exceptional-case evidence (section 19 — all five preconditions completed):**

```text
FAILED_ENDPOINT=https://api.github.com/user
HTTP_STATUS=401
SANITIZED_GITHUB_ERROR=Bad credentials
RUNTIME_BINDING=PASS
PLACEHOLDER_DETECTED=NO
```

The placeholder was never the only problem — it was masking a second one. With the placeholder physically deleted, the runtime restarted, and the REAL decrypted 40-character `ghp_` token correctly bound and sent to GitHub from the production worker itself, GitHub still returns **401 Bad credentials**. That is GitHub declaring the token `ghp_****QTOt` (saved 2026-07-10) **revoked at GitHub's side**. No resolver, restart, or code path can revive a token GitHub has invalidated.

This is the single remaining blocker for push → deploy → QA of every queued fix. Per your own section 19 criteria, a replacement credential request is now justified: paste a new GitHub token (repo scope, for `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`) directly in chat — the moment it arrives I will encrypt it into the owner store, verify auth/push permission, push commit 70e0af2, deploy, and run the full production QA in one uninterrupted sequence.
